reason: FUTURE-DIRECTEDNESS GAP: Definition 1 (def:timelike-worldline, line 45-51 of lecA1.tex) defines a timelike worldline as a smooth embedding gamma:I->R^4 with eta_{ab} dot-gamma^a dot-gamma^b < 0. With signature (-,+,+,+), this gives -(dot-gamma^0)^2 + |dot-gamma_spatial|^2 < 0, which implies |dot-gamma^0| > |dot-gamma_spatial|, but does NOT fix the sign of dot-gamma^0. The curve could be past-directed (dot-gamma^0 < 0). However, the proof of Proposition 1 (line 87-88) asserts 'the time component gamma_j^0(tau) satisfies d-gamma_j^0/d-tau > 0 (future-directed)' as if this follows from timelikeness alone. It does not: future-directedness is an additional hypothesis not present in Definition 1. The proof should either (a) add 'future-directed' to Definition 1 explicitly, or (b) note that one may reparametrise tau -> -tau to achieve dot-gamma^0 > 0 WLOG. Without this, the inference 'timelike => dot-gamma^0 > 0' in line 88 is invalid.
